ddls:
CREATE TABLE "requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint being requested */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the request in milliseconds */,
  "time_requested" TEXT /* Timestamp when the request was made */,
  "version_requested" INTEGER /* Version of the API being requested */,
  "group_by" TEXT /* Group by parameter for the request */,
  "ip" TEXT /* IP address of the client making the request */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* Status code of the response */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the request */,
  "request_method" TEXT /* HTTP method of the request */,
  "request_uri" TEXT /* Full URI of the request */,
  "request_size" INTEGER /* Size of the request in bytes */,
  "response_size" INTEGER /* Size of the response in bytes */,
  "load_time" REAL /* Time taken to load the response */,
  "retry_count" INTEGER /* Number of retries for the request */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the request fails */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_version_requested FOREIGN KEY ("version_requested") REFERENCES versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_status_code FOREIGN KEY ("status_code") REFERENCES status_codes ("status_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_group_by FOREIGN KEY ("group_by") REFERENCES group_bys ("group_by_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_ip FOREIGN KEY ("ip") REFERENCES ips ("ip_address")
)

CREATE TABLE "endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each endpoint */,
  "endpoint_name" TEXT /* Name of the endpoint */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the endpoint */,
  "method" TEXT /* HTTP method of the endpoint (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) */,
  "path" TEXT /* Full path of the endpoint */,
  "rate_limit" INTEGER /* Rate limit for the endpoint */,
  "timeout" REAL /* Timeout for the endpoint */,
  "authentication_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if authentication is required for the endpoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the version */,
  "deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the version is deprecated */,
  "end_of_support" TEXT /* End-of-support date for the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "group_bys" (
  "group_by_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each group by parameter */,
  "group_by_name" TEXT /* Name of the group by parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the group by parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_by_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ips" (
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each IP address */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the client */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the client's location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the client's location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the client's location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "status_codes" (
  "status_code_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status code */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* Status code of the response */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_code_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, DevOps engineer, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the user (e.g., JSON array of permissions) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* ID of the endpoint being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the endpoint */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the endpoint was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Exact timestamp of the access event */,
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* ID of the IP address of the user accessing the endpoint */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_ip_id FOREIGN KEY ("ip_id") REFERENCES ips ("ip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_endpoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint_id") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_bodies" (
  "request_body_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request body */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request the body belongs to */,
  "body" TEXT /* Request body */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Content type of the request body (e.g., JSON, XML) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_body_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_bodies_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_bodies" (
  "response_body_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response body */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request the body belongs to */,
  "body" TEXT /* Response body */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Content type of the response body (e.g., JSON, XML) */,
  "response_time" REAL /* Time taken to generate the response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_body_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_response_bodies_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "headers" (
  "header_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each header */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request the header belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the header */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the header */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("header_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_headers_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id")
)
